title: `links reorder` — reorder links in links.yaml
description: Implement the `links reorder` command. Allows moving individual links to a new position or re-specifying the full order by label. Works on the links array in links.yaml.

features: Implement src/commands/reorder.js with the following interface:
`links reorder` (no args) — print current link order with 1-based indices: '1. GitHub', '2. Twitter', etc. Hint: use links move to reorder.
`links reorder move <label> <position>` — move the named link to the given 1-based position, shifting others accordingly. Example: `links reorder move Twitter 1` makes Twitter the first link.
`links reorder up <label>` — move the named link one position up (lower index). No-op if already first, with message: '<label> is already at the top.'
`links reorder down <label>` — move the named link one position down (higher index). No-op if already last.

`links reorder set <label1> <label2> ...` — specify the complete new order by passing all labels as arguments. Validates that all existing labels are present (no omissions allowed — prevents accidental deletion). Errors if any label is missing or unknown.
All subcommands: label matching is case-insensitive. If a label is not found, exit 1 with: 'No link with label <label> found.'
After any mutation, write updated config via writeConfig() and print the new order summary.

notes: Move algorithm for `move <label> <pos>`: splice the item out, then insert at (pos-1). For `set`, validate all current labels appear in the args before rewriting — this prevents silent data loss. Position arg is 1-indexed for user-friendliness.
